goal: Freeze the next-phase lane shape, priorities, non-goals, and architecture mapping after Mermaid 11.15 structural parity completion.
handoff: DONE. Lane shape now maps the 2026-06-01 architecture audit to bounded tasks, records the honest post-11.15 residual front, and freezes the execution order: truth/governance, then shared seams, then deep audits/family rubric.

goal: Create a single Mermaid baseline registry/provenance seam and stop presenting stale 11.12-era baseline names as current truth across production surfaces.
handoff: DONE. Added merman-core baseline truth module, moved Engine/default registry construction to pinned-baseline constructors, routed importer/bench/xtask live surfaces away from default_mermaid_11_12_2*, and made the legacy generated suffix explicit historical provenance instead of active baseline identity.

goal: Create and apply a residual taxonomy for parity-root and related residual work.
validation: top active residual buckets classified in docs
handoff: DONE. Residual governance now uses a durable six-class taxonomy and the active root residual lane maps Flowchart/Architecture/Sequence/Class/Timeline/Journey buckets into it without pretending the counts are completion percentages.

goal: Extract reusable measurement/root-bounds seams so future parity fixes stop embedding hidden browser-like bbox heuristics across diagram code.
validation: no-growth review of ad hoc constants
handoff: DONE. Moved svg_emitted_bounds to svg/parity root-bounds infrastructure, centralized Sequence note final wrap/measure across layout/root/render, and verified no override growth. The long leftOf note root-width residual remains open and should not be overfit.

goal: Audit manatee/dugong-adjacent layout behavior through source-backed input-model seams, starting with Architecture residual families.
validation: focused Architecture residual evidence with per-seam outcomes
handoff: IN_PROGRESS. Architecture FCoSE adapter and root-bounds work now distinguishes node BoundsExtras, edge-label createText transforms, SVG group bbox padding, source-checkout authority, and Cytoscape bbox phases without forcing residuals closed. Architecture structural parity remains green and parity-root now has 25 mismatches after the isolated top-level service root-bounds seam. Dugong-adjacent audit is source-backed: repo-ref/dagre and repo-ref/graphlib are pinned, Graphlib has a coverage ledger, exposed helper algorithm tests are ported, public Graph API source-test slices cover current consumer-relevant basics/queries/children-root/setPath-label/setNodes/parent/setEdge-edgeKey invariants, and Rust/JS API-shape differences remain explicit. A follow-up Graphlib JSON seam slice is now landed: dugong_graphlib::json::{write,read} mirrors upstream graphlib.json.write/read with Option-label undefined semantics, all six json-test.js cases are ported, and explicit JSON null stays distinct from omitted undefined labels; default-collapsing helpers are an explicit Rust bridge. Implemented-matrix structural parity stayed green after this container-only slice. A consumer follow-up now routes xtask's Dagre reference input, JS output, and Rust output artifacts through the Graphlib JSON options/value/nodes/edges shape, with compare-dagre-layout basic still zero-delta. ARCH-022's first Dagre reference adapter slice is landed: dagre_reference.rs owns the Rust-side reference schema, JS harness invocation, compound-edge normalization, Rust snapshots, and delta extraction, while compare-dagre-layout remains a State-only graph producer. Architecture Cytoscape service-label measurement now flows through explicit ArchitectureCytoscapeChildLabelBounds and ArchitectureCytoscapeChildContributionBounds seams, so FCoSE/SVG root/group estimation keeps SVG createText measurement separate from Cytoscape child body/label/union phases. A disconnected-islands audit rejected globally using cytoscape_group_child_bounds for top-level services: it fixed one height-only row but expanded full Architecture root mismatches from 26 to 84. The first narrow phase-specific follow-up now uses the Cytoscape child union only for isolated top-level services in diagrams that also have groups, making that row exact and reducing full Architecture root mismatches from 26 to 25. A later child-contribution seam removed the old single cytoscape_group_child_bounds code field in favor of ArchitectureCytoscapeChildContributionBounds { body_bounds, label_bounds, union_bounds }; structural parity stayed green and parity-root remained the existing 25-mismatch queue. A follow-up FCoSE contribution seam now derives node BoundsExtras from the same expanded-body, optional-label, and union contribution vocabulary without changing Architecture output. Continue residual-driven audits; do not start a broad solver rewrite, fake browser-bound parity, or implement unused Graphlib algorithms without a Mermaid/Dagre consumer.

goal: Pilot a deeper one-semantic-truth direction for one high-value diagram family to reduce typed-vs-JSON parallel master paths.
validation: Sequence compatibility JSON now projects from SequenceDiagramRenderModel
handoff: DONE. Sequence was selected as the pilot because it already had a typed render model but still carried a separate JSON master path in SequenceDb::into_model. The compatibility JSON adapter now lives on SequenceDiagramRenderModel::to_compat_json(...), SequenceDb::into_model delegates through into_render_model(), and the typed-vs-JSON parse test covers actors, boxes, messages, notes, create/destroy indexes, omitted placement, and omitted centralConnection. This does not claim repo-wide semantic unification or Sequence root-measurement closure; Sequence structural parity remains green while root parity still reports existing measurement residuals.

goal: Define a durable rubric and priority order for unsupported Mermaid families.
handoff: DONE. Added a pinned-source admission rubric for unsupported Mermaid families and corrected the 11.15 status table. Priority order is treeView, ishikawa, eventmodeling, venn, wardley. Venn is blocked on a source-backed @upsetjs/venn.js layout plan, Wardley is a large family lane, and railroad/cynefin are absent from pinned Mermaid 11.15 source so they are not part of the 11.15 parity backlog.

goal: Find and fix functional rendering defects that DOM structural parity can miss, especially missing Mermaid 11.15 diagram-specific CSS/theme emission that makes output unreadable or loses semantic color cues.
validation: manual render evidence for user-provided repros
handoff: IN_PROGRESS. Multiple source-backed slices fixed missing or stale theme/CSS emission across the implemented matrix, added a theme coverage ledger, corrected Mermaid 11.15 official theme names, exposed opt-in duplicate native/fallback cleanup through Rust pipelines plus shared binding options_json, re-audited Zed PR 57967 as host palette policy rather than default merman theme behavior, added binding options_json.site_config so non-Rust hosts can pass Mermaid theme/themeVariables/themeCSS defaults without source directives, added binding svg.scoped_css/css_override_policy so hosts can inject scoped palette CSS explicitly while resvg-safe pipelines sanitize injected CSS after insertion, and added opt-in root background rewriting through RootBackgroundPostprocessor plus binding svg.root_background_color after reconciling Mermaid source vs capture output. A source-checkout audit restored repo-ref/mermaid from accidental develop drift to the lockfile Mermaid 11.15 commit and confirmed the theme coverage ledger still matches pinned provider inventory. A Zed feedback follow-up confirmed common theme needs are covered and fixed a separate deep Flowchart subgraph stack-overflow risk by moving cluster traversals to explicit stacks. A resvg-safe fixture smoke follow-up added a public API host-integration gate for the user Kanban/GitGraph examples, dark-theme Flowchart, representative supported-family fixtures, invalid-token checks, and optional PNG rasterization. The raster ink gate now rejects contentful all-background PNG output, is calibrated for non-visual parser/header/options fixtures, and found/fixed a single-leaf Treemap readable-label defect caused by Mermaid's transparent first cell fill plus white generated label color. A follow-up directive-only calibration fixed false raster-ink failures for State classDef-only, State floating-note parser-only, and Flowchart click-only fixtures without changing renderer output; full State and split-prefix Flowchart raster audits passed. A boundary renderability gate now covers info/error/zenuml fixtures separately from the supported-family style-provider matrix, including lenient suppressed-error rendering and raster ink checks. A follow-up Mindmap look/theme seam slice now fixes the source-backed getData/render seam: Mindmap nodes and edges carry configured Mermaid look instead of hardcoded "default", default snapshots use "classic", redux themes select the upstream rounded default shape, and current SVG emits data-look="neo" plus matching neo CSS/gradient defs only for neo output while structural parity stays green. Continue by scanning supported diagrams for blank output, hidden labels, black blocks, missing semantic colors, crashes, and parsed-but-not-emitted diagram style options before returning to fine root viewport residuals.

goal: Prepare the Mermaid 11.15 baseline corpus before further parity work by classifying stale current-facing baseline surfaces, deciding whether to refresh all stored upstream SVGs, and defining official fixture/test admission gates.
validation: all-family upstream baseline check or documented diagnostics
layout snapshot and renderability smoke checks for changed families
handoff: IN_PROGRESS. Info visible output and Info stored baselines are on v11.15.0. Error visible version text plus xtask gap/import report labels now route through pinned baseline truth. Per-family upstream SVG inventory shows no all-baseline refresh is needed. Requirement upstream SVGs are refreshed and the local renderer now matches Mermaid 11.15 current DOM under compare-requirement-svgs --check-dom --dom-mode parity --dom-decimals 3. Block upstream SVGs/layout goldens are refreshed and the local renderer now matches Mermaid 11.15 current DOM under compare-block-svgs --check-dom --dom-mode parity --dom-decimals 3. Gantt upstream SVGs are refreshed, the missing zed_pr_57644_gantt layout golden is added, and the local renderer now matches Mermaid 11.15 current DOM under compare-gantt-svgs --check-dom --dom-mode parity --dom-decimals 3. Kanban upstream SVGs are refreshed and the local renderer now matches Mermaid 11.15 current DOM under compare-kanban-svgs --check-dom --dom-mode parity --dom-decimals 3 after diagram-prefixed section/item DOM ids and current markdown title label spans. Mindmap upstream SVGs are refreshed, the missing zed_pr_57644_mindmap layout golden is added, and the local renderer now matches Mermaid 11.15 current DOM under compare-mindmap-svgs --check-dom --dom-mode parity --dom-decimals 3 after root shadow defs, explicit classic data-look, diagram-prefixed node/edge DOM ids, margin markers, section class cycle wrapping, direct classic rounded/hexagon DOM, and dead rough helper cleanup. Refresh remaining broad stale family radar; point-refresh class, timeline, and Flowchart HTML demo KaTeX drift; then rerun layout, compare, and renderability gates. No broad official fixture import is indicated yet.
